notes: SHIP v1.14.251 — MEDIA CONV: the app can finally NAME a media converter. Closes the honesty arc that started at .238. John's field call 2026-07-14: 'net-6x100g-02 is a media converter, fs-media-converter-chassis too.' The app had NO media-converter type (gpu/switch/pdu/patch/server/storage/unknown/blank), so those 11 hosts sat as honest-but-useless gold UNKNOWN since .246. ⚠️ NAMING + COLOUR ARE MINE, NOT JOHN'S — RECORDED AS SUCH. John ruled the HARDWARE and explicitly DELEGATED the rest ('you do whats best', 2026-07-14). He did NOT specify the type key, the label, or the colour. If he dislikes any of it, it is one token. THE DESIGN CALL: NOT a switch — a media converter does not switch, and typing it as one would be exactly the class of lie .246 exists to kill. NOT patch either — a patch panel is PASSIVE, a media converter is POWERED ACTIVE gear. So it gets its own type: key `media`, label `MEDIA CONV`, colour #2ee6a8, deliberately held APART from patch teal #1fffd0 because patch is the type it sits closest to conceptually and is therefore the pair a gloved tech is likeliest to confuse at arm's length. FOUR EDITS: (1) TYPE_COLOR + media:'#2ee6a8'. (2) TLABEL + media:'MEDIA CONV'. (3) _TMAP + media:'media' (bridges master_hostType's new return into the Forge tray). (4) master_hostType gains TWO rules at the TAIL. ⭐ TWO RULES, NOT ONE — THEY REST ON DIFFERENT KINDS OF EVIDENCE AND MUST NOT BE COLLAPSED: (a) SELF-NAMING, `/media[\s-]*conv/` -> `fs-media-converter-chassis` SAYS WHAT IT IS IN ITS OWN MODEL STRING. Reading a device's name is not an inference, so a GENERAL rule is legitimate and a future `*-media-converter-*` rev is covered on the same evidence (same class as .245's generic /ufm|fabric\s*manager/). (b) OWNER'S WORD ONLY, `/(^|[^a-z0-9-])net-6x100g-02([^a-z0-9-]|$)/` -> the NetBox slug `net-6x100g-02` does NOT say what the device is; nothing in the string hints 'media converter'. John's ruling is the ENTIRE evidence, so it is MODEL-EXACT, exactly like .247's q3400-ra. A future -03/-021 rev is different hardware until he rules on it. Boundary is the negated class, NOT \b — per .250, \b matches on BOTH sides of a hyphen and would leak net-6x100g-02-x and net-6x100g-021. Both rules sit at the TAIL so neither can steal a host an earlier, more specific classifier already claimed (same discipline as the UFM rule). ⚠️ TYPE ONLY — HEIGHTS UNTOUCHED AND NOT INFERRED FROM THE TYPE RULING (the .247 line, paying off again): net-6x100g-02 is 1U KNOWN (seeded .242, boundary-fixed .249) and draws TO SCALE; fs-media-converter-chassis is NOT in MASTER_U_TABLE, so it reads MEDIA CONV *and still carries the gold hazard hatch + 'MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN'*. Reading MEDIA CONV while hatched is CORRECT, not a bug — John ruled WHAT they are, not HOW TALL. GUARD (both real masters replayed through the SHIPPED function vs the .250 baseline): exactly 11 hosts move, ALL `unknown -> media`, and the ONLY models that move are the two he ruled. ZERO other hosts change type on either master. HEIGHT DRIFT = 0 across all 6490 placed hosts. ⭐ SPARKS NOW HAS ZERO UNKNOWN-TYPE HOSTS (was 459 = 11.1% before .246). DFW02 retains exactly 1 unknown — its empty-model row @c1:001:38, which is still owed John's ruling. .246 INVARIANTS RE-CHECKED AND GREEN: every _TMAP value resolves to BOTH a defined TLABEL and a defined TYPE_COLOR (0 undefined-label rows — the failure that would print the literal word 'undefined' on a tray). Over-catch probes PASS: `media-server` -> unknown (the generic rule does not grab anything merely containing 'media'), `net-6x100g-021`/`-02-x`/`-03` -> unknown, `q3400-ra` -> still switch. `fs-media-converter-chassis-v2` -> media (intended: self-naming). ⚠️ PRE-EXISTING, NOT FIXED (re-flagged from .246, John's call): TYPE_COLOR.pdu and TYPE_COLOR.unknown SHARE #ffcb45 — a PDU spine and an UNKNOWN spine are indistinguishable by colour alone (label + flag still differ). MEDIA does not participate in that collision. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS brace balance 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS this ship), CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0), three-stamp lockstep .250 -> .251. ?legacy=1: unaffected — master_hostType is a pure classifier and TYPE_COLOR/TLABEL/_TMAP are display-only inside forge3d_render; no markup, no gating, no CSS. PERSISTENCE UNCHANGED: mscope_buildRacksFromSnapshot persists master_hostType's RAW return, and 'media' is a new raw value — but _TMAP is display-only and deploy_classifyDevice (the PERSISTED EDP/CSV path) is untouched, so no seeded deployment record changes shape. BATCH: John reviewed the .245-.250 stack ('i looked', 2026-07-14) and directed continuation ('keep cooking'). ⚠️ RECORDED HONESTLY: that is an OWNER ACKNOWLEDGEMENT, **not** an itemized checklist pass — the per-ship items in INTEGRATION-STATE §21 were not walked one by one in front of me. This ship opens a NEW batch at 1. DEVICE-VERIFY (John): [.251] SPARKS s3:176 -> U31 metal-jump01 / U29 pkey02 / U28 pkey04 now read **MEDIA CONV** in green (#2ee6a8), drawn solid + to scale, NO hatch (they are 1U-known). The 5 fs-media-converter-chassis rows (s3:011/s3:111/s1:010/s2:060/s3:030) read **MEDIA CONV** *and still carry the gold hatch + MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN* — both at once is correct. No tray prints 'undefined'. RACKED/PENDING counts unchanged. DFW02 completely unchanged. STILL OWED, JOHN'S RULING: DFW02's one empty-model row @c1:001:38 (the last unknown on either master) · the HEIGHT of q3400-ra (448 hosts still hatched; SPARKS' uniform 5U pitch BOUNDS it at <=5U but does not determine it) · the HEIGHT of fs-media-converter-chassis (5 hosts, now typed but still hatched) · whether the pdu/unknown gold collision matters.
